name: EEOICPA
fullName: Energy Employees Occupational Illness Compensation Program Act
shortDefinition: A federal program that provides covered medical care — including home health — at no out-of-pocket cost to approved Department of Energy and Nevada Test Site workers who became ill from their work.
eligibilitySummary: Nevada Test Site workers and DOE facility employees with an accepted condition (or consequential condition approved by the Department of Labor).

name: OWCP / FECA
fullName: Office of Workers' Compensation Programs (Federal Employees' Compensation Act)
shortDefinition: Federal workers' compensation that may cover physician-prescribed home health services at no out-of-pocket cost when an injury or illness is accepted and care is authorized.
eligibilitySummary: Federal employees and postal (USPS) workers with an OWCP-accepted, work-related injury or illness, with services prescribed by a physician and authorized by OWCP.

name: Veterans / VA Community Care
shortDefinition: A VA program that authorizes eligible veterans to receive home health services from approved community providers when VA facilities cannot readily provide the care.
eligibilitySummary: VA-enrolled veterans with a referral/authorization and a provider order; ~240,000 veterans in the VA Southern Nevada Healthcare System.
